summary: P0-1 artifact freshness debt now has noncanonical raw-intake notes excluded, reusable raw-intake schemas attached, and safe-to-backfill envelope/currentness artifacts normalized.

observations.problem: Two raw-intake run-local JSON notes had no reusable producer contract but were counted as missing schema, missing envelope, and unknown currentness debt.
observations.why_it_matters: Creating schemas for one-off audit notes would reduce counts while weakening the signal of stable artifact debt; canonical debt should point at reusable producer contracts.
observations.automation_candidate: Keep an explicit noncanonical archived-run-note allowlist for one-off audit JSON, and require remaining raw-intake JSON debt to be handled by real shared schemas or provenance-backed archive copy contracts.
observations.suggested_followup: Completed through the follow-on P0-1 tranches: reusable raw-intake schemas are attached, missing_schema_count is 0, and the safe-to-backfill missing envelope/currentness tranche is closed. Remaining missing envelope/currentness debt is mtime-sensitive and should be handled by regeneration, archive classification, or producer-specific timestamp refresh rather than blind backfill.
